finding: the Quran offers an explicit, integrative methodology: observe the signs (in text, nature, history, self) and reason/reflect/remember. It is not a single-source method.
process: observe → recognize signs → reason/reflect → remember; grounded in text, nature, history, and the human self simultaneously

reconstructed_methodology.integration: text (كتاب) + nature + history (story/عبرت) + reason + observation, integrated
reconstructed_methodology.step_1_observe: the Quran commands observation (نظر، بصر، سمع، سير) — 82 imperative tokens
reconstructed_methodology.step_2_recognize_signs: it presents آیات (signs) in nature, history, and the self — 353 sign-ayahs
reconstructed_methodology.step_3_reason: it commands and repeats reasoning/reflection (عقل، فكر، دبر) — most-repeated method 'علم'
reconstructed_methodology.step_4_remember: it frames understanding as ذكر (remembrance) and presents itself as ذكر/هدى
